w:r.w:t: Smart Online Food Ordering and Restaurant Management System
Smart Online Food Ordering and Restaurant Management System

The Smart Online Food Ordering and Restaurant Management System is a web-based application designed to enable customers to order food online and allow restaurants to manage operations digitally. The system replaces traditional phone-based and manual order
processing methods with an automated, secure, and efficient platform.
This project plan defines the objectives, scope, methodology, timeline, resources, risks, and quality control procedures required for successful project completion.
The objectives of this project are:
To design and develop a web-based food ordering system.
To implement secure user registration and login functionality.
To allow customers to browse menu categories and items.
To provide shopping cart and order placement features.
To integrate secure online payment functionality.
To develop an administrative dashboard for managing orders and menu items.
To ensure the system is secure, reliable, and user-friendly.
The following deliverables will be provided:
System design diagrams including Use Case diagram, ERD, and DFD
System requirements will be gathered, analyzed, and documented. Both functional and non-functional requirements will be defined clearly.
System architecture, database schema, and user interface design will be prepared. System diagrams wil
l be developed during this phase.
Front-end and back-end development will be completed. The application will be integrated with the database.
The system will undergo unit testing, integration testing, and system testing to en
The completed system will be deployed for demonstration and evaluation.
Necessary corrections and improvements will be applied after testing and supervisor feedback.
The project duration is five months, structured as follows:
System testing, documentation, and final submission
Follow a strict schedule and hold regular supervisor meetings.
Conduct regular testing and debugging throughout the development lifecycle.
Use trusted, well-documented payment APIs (e.g., Stripe, PayPal).
Implement password encryption and secure authentication mechanisms.

The system will be tested using unit testing, integration testing, and system testing methods. User acceptance testing (UAT) will be conducted before final submission. All documentation will be reviewed to ensure compliance with academic standards.
A fully functional web-based food ordering system
Improved efficiency in restaurant order management
This project plan presents a structure

d approach for developing the Smart Online Food Ordering and Restaurant Management System. By following the defined objectives, scope, methodology, and timeline, the project is expected to be completed successfully and meet both academic and practical requ
